instrument.model: Adiabatic-calorimeter Cp(T) measurement (1-50 K, B up to 9 T) (literature extraction)

sample_provenance.batch_lot: Bouquet/Fisher/Phillips/Hinks/Jorgensen (LBNL+Argonne 2001) isotopically-enriched Mg-11B2 polycrystal — sealed Ta tube reaction

provenance.source_citation: F. Bouquet, R. A. Fisher, N. E. Phillips, D. G. Hinks, J. D. Jorgensen, 'Specific Heat of Mg-11B2: Evidence for a Second Energy Gap', Physical Review Letters 87, 047001 (2001), arXiv:cond-mat/0104206. Reports gamma_n = 2.6 mJ/mol/K^2, Theta_Debye = 1050 K, deltaCp(Tc) = 133 mJ/mol/K at Tc = 38.7 K.

scope_caveats: Digitized from published Cp(T) figure (Bouquet et al. arXiv:cond-mat/0104206 Fig. 1, 2); values ±5%.
deltaCp/(gamma * Tc) = 133/(2.6 * 38.7) = 1.32 — slightly BELOW the BCS weak-coupling prediction 1.43 (Bouquet attributes this to a second small gap; consistent with MgB2 two-band SC).
Sample is isotopically-enriched Mg-11B2 (not natural-abundance MgB2); Tc = 38.7 K is slightly shifted vs natural-MgB2 Tc=39.0 K via the boron isotope effect.
Single-sample literature value; replication count reflects independent MgB2 Cp reproductions (Wang/Plackowski/Junod 2001 etc.), not within-lot repeats.
Cp values are order-of-magnitude estimates from digitized figure traces; the deltaCp(Tc)=133 mJ/mol/K is the quoted paper value (not digitized).
citations: Bouquet, Fisher, Phillips, Hinks, Jorgensen, PRL 87, 047001 (2001), arXiv:cond-mat/0104206
Finnemore, Ostenson, Bud'ko, Lapertot, Canfield, PRL 86, 2420 (2001), arXiv:cond-mat/0102114 — independent thermo confirmation
RTSC.md §8.2 (MgB2 family) + §8.4 (BCS specific-heat jump falsifier row)
Tinkham, 'Introduction to Superconductivity', 2nd ed., §3.4 (BCS weak-coupling deltaCp/gamma*Tc = 1.43)
